messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Download stats are currently unavailable
messages.body_plain:
Hi Lori,

The download stats are very valuable, and needed by Bioconductor developers for many types of grant applications, so I hope a resolution can be found. Any idea of likely timeframe?

Regards
Gordon

--------------------------------------------
Professor Gordon K Smyth
School of Mathematics & Statistics, Uni of Melbourne
Lab Head, Technological Sciences Division, WEHI
htttps://gksmyth.github.io

> We appreciate your patience.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] bioconductor docker image update to R 4.6.1?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] bioconductor docker image update to R 4.6.1?
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

When is the bioconductor docker image expected to be updated with R 4.6.1?
I'm getting a build error for SeqVarTools in both release and devel that
may be related to the 4.6.0 -> 4.6.1 transition, but I can't reproduce it
since bioconductor/bioconductor_docker:devel is still on 4.6.0.

thanks,
Stephanie

-- 

Stephanie M. Gogarten, PhD

Senior Research Scientist / Genetic Analysis Center
<https://www.biostat.washington.edu/research/centers/gac>

Department of Biostatistics <https://www.biostat.washington.edu/>

UNIVERSITY OF WASHINGTON



UW Tower Box 359461

4333 Brooklyn Ave NE, Seattle, WA 98195

206.221.0757

sdmorris at uw.edu

she/her/hers

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] bioconductor docker image update to R 4.6.1?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] bioconductor docker image update to R 4.6.1?
messages.body_plain:
Hi Stephanie,

Thanks for making us aware of this.

I've kicked off builds for the devel image and I'm currently working on 
the release one.

They should be done within a day or so.

Best regards,

Marcel

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] excluderanges data update
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

We are looking to update our data package
https://bioconductor.org/packages/excluderanges/. This package has 82
objects, available via AnnotationHub. All objects have been updated. The
metadata is unchanged.

The updated objects would be better available in the next release of
Bioconductor. The old objects can remain unchanged for reproducibility, but
from the new release it's better to use the new objects. Ideally, using the
same AnnotationHub IDs, but if new need to be created, that is OK.

Thanks,
Mikhail

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

metadat

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Workshop at STAI-X 26 (July 31, Harvard University)
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Workshop at STAI-X 26 (July 31, Harvard University)
messages.body_plain:
A Bioconductor-focused workshop will take place on Friday, July 31, 2026, at Harvard University, the day before JSM begins in Boston.

AI for the Bioconductor Ecosystem: Developers, Infrastructure, and Community

This workshop is part of STAI-X 26 and will include morning and afternoon sessions exploring AI for the Bioconductor ecosystem, including developer tools, infrastructure, and community topics.

Registration

  *   Attendance at the Bioconductor workshop only is free. Registration for the full STAI-X 26 conference is optional.
  *   Breakfast and lunch will be provided
  *   The workshop is in person only
  *   Space is limited

Workshop information: https://statsupai.org/STAIX2026/index.html

If you think you may attend, please complete this form to assist with planning: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScFbdc5EZHSD-PC_8bO1GEUMRSrXS3ccEV2PpNvBRvl12osBA/viewform

Best wishes,

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *
BioC2026 (10-12 August, Seattle, USA)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>
  *
BioCAsia 2026 (19-20 November, Melbourne, Australia)<https://biocasia2026.bioconductor.org>
  *
EuroBioC2027 (8-10 September, Basel, Switzerland)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Board Nominations Open
messages.body_plain:
Nominations are now open for the 2026 Bioconductor Community and Technical Advisory Boards!

If you're interested in contributing to the Bioconductor project, or know someone who would be a great fit, please consider applying or nominating.

Community Advisory Board (CAB):
  *   Promote inclusive participation
  *   Support the user and developer community

Technical Advisory Board (TAB):
  *   Guide technical direction
  *   Contribute to long-term sustainability strategies

Deadline: August 31, 2026

Apply here:
CAB: https://forms.gle/qXwPqEzPET45MimW7
TAB: https://forms.gle/MLYBYDgdbU1CUVU99



Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] July Developer Forum
messages.body_plain:
Hi folks,

Our July Developer Forum will be held on Thursday July 30th at 4pm UTC! We will have Aaron Lun presenting on:

The Orchestrating Single Cell Analysis books

The Orchestrating Single Cell Analysis books are some of Bioconductor's most widely used resources for learning about single-cell analyses. However, maintenance has been challenging for a variety of reasons. This talk will focus on the design decisions behind the current book configuration, the ensuing challenges to their continued maintenance, and some paths forward for more sustainable book development in Bioconductor.

A registration link is included here:

https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/qtozL2LDSkK2-7eXxFdlVw

If you would like to present at an upcoming developer forum, or request a specific topic, please reach out to me.

Nicholas Cooley, PhD (he/him)
Developer Engagement Lead
Bioconductor / University of Limerick






	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioCAsia 2026 workshop proposal call now open
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] BioCAsia 2026 workshop proposal call now open
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

The call for workshop proposals for BioCAsia 2026, taking place 19-20 November 2026 in Melbourne, Australia, is now open.

We encourage proposals on Bioconductor software and workflows, as well as topics such as single-cell and spatial omics, reproducible workflows, cloud computing, machine learning, genomics, microbiome analysis, and related areas.

Submission deadline: 21 August 2026

Details and proposal submission: https://www.abacbs.org/workshops-2026-call

Best wishes,

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager
School of Medicine,
University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland
Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *
BioC2026 (10-12 August, Seattle, USA)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>
  *
BioCAsia 2026 (19-20 November, Melbourne, Australia)<https://biocasia2026.bioconductor.org>
  *
EuroBioC2027 (8-10 September, Basel, Switzerland)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>


I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to allow force push for TraianProt (reduce repository size)
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Request to allow force push for TraianProt (reduce repository size)
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I have recently cleaned up the Git history of my package *TraianProt* using
git-filter-repo to remove large files (such as heavy PDFs, HTMLs, and raw
data files) and comply with the 5 MB repository size limit.

The repository size is now successfully reduced to *2.22 MiB*.

However, when trying to push the clean history to the Bioconductor server
using git push upstream main:devel --force, the hook declined the push (DENIED
by fallthru).

Could you please temporarily enable force-push permissions on the
git.bioconductor.org:packages/TraianProt repository for my user (
*SamueldelaCamaraFuentes*) so I can push the clean history?

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,

Samuel
-- 

Samuel de la C?mara Fuentes

CAI de T?cnicas Biol?gicas

Unidad de Prote?mica (UCM)

Facultad de Farmacia. Universidad Complutense de Madrid

Pza. Ram?n y Cajal S/N
28040 Madrid

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

git-filter-repo to remove large files (such as heavy PDFs, HTMLs, and raw
data files) and comp

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of SAMUEL DE LA CAMARA FUENTES via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Thursday, July 16, 2026 9:18 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to allow force push for TraianProt (reduce repository size)

Dear Bioconductor Core Team,

I have recently cleaned up the Git history of my package *TraianProt* using
git-filter-repo to remove large files (such as heavy PDFs, HTMLs, and raw
data files) and comply with the 5 MB repository size limit.

The repository size is now successfully reduced to *2.22 MiB*.

However, when trying to push the clean history to the Bioconductor server
using git push upstream main:devel --force, the hook declined the push (DENIED
by fallthru).

Could you please temporarily enable force-push permissions on the
git.bioconductor.org:packages/TraianProt repository for my user (
*SamueldelaCamaraFuentes*) so I can push the clean history?

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,

Samuel
--

Samuel de la C?mara Fuentes

CAI de T?cnicas Biol?gicas

Unidad de Prote?mica (UCM)

Facultad de Farmacia. Universidad Complutense de Madrid

Pza. Ram?n y Cajal S/N
28040 Madrid
